name: Robledo de Chavela, Valdemorillo, Navalagamella – circular route
name_original: Robledo de Chavela, Valdemorillo, Navalagamella – ruta circular
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Robledo De Chavela are situated in the Sierra Oeste of Madrid, nestled between the Guadarrama and Gredos mountain ranges. The region features varied topography, including significant mountainous terrain with peaks like El Almojón and San Benito. Cyclists can expect diverse ecosystems, including extensive oak and stone pine forests, dehesas, and ash groves, with riparian vegetation along the Cofio River. This landscape provides a range of road cycling terrain, from moderate climbs to more strenuous ascents. Best road cycling routes around Robledo De Chavela The most popular road cycling route is Robledo de Chavela, Valdemorillo, Navalagamella – circular route, a 36.0 miles (58.0 km) trail that takes 2 hours 50 minutes to complete, offering a circular journey through varied landscapes. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Cruz Verde Pass – La Escondida loop from Robledo de Chavela, a difficult 68.8 miles (110.7 km) path known for its significant elevation changes, including the challenging Cruz Verde Pass. Local road cyclists also love the Views of Hoyo de Pinares – Puerto de la Lancha loop from Robledo de Chavela, a 91.5 miles (147.2 km) trail leading through mountainous terrain and offering expansive views, often completed in about 7 hours 31 minutes. Road cycling around Robledo De Chavela is defined by mountainous terrain, oak and stone pine forests, and riparian vegetation along the Cofio River. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to difficult. The routes in Robledo De Chavela are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.25 stars from over 7 reviews. More than 500 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Robledo De Chavela's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes around Robledo De Chavela?
A: The region offers varied topography, situated between the Guadarrama and Gredos mountain ranges. You'll encounter significant mountainous terrain with peaks like El Almojón and San Benito, as well as diverse ecosystems including oak and stone pine forests, dehesas, and riparian vegetation along the Cofio River. Routes range from moderate climbs to more strenuous ascents.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners or more advanced cyclists?
A: While many routes feature significant elevation changes, there are options for different ability levels. The area has 9 moderate routes and 12 difficult routes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e977182165/robledo-de-chavela-valdemorillo-navalagamella-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Robledo de Chavela, Valdemorillo, Navalagamella – circular route&lt;/a&gt; is considered moderate, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31521543" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cruz Verde Pass – La Escondida loop from Robledo de Chavela&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging option with substantial elevation gain.

Q: How long do road cycling routes typically take in this area?
A: Route durations vary significantly based on distance and difficulty. For instance, a moderate route like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e977182165/robledo-de-chavela-valdemorillo-navalagamella-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Robledo de Chavela, Valdemorillo, Navalagamella – circular route&lt;/a&gt; can take around 2 hours 50 minutes, while more difficult and longer routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6928874" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views of Hoyo de Pinares – Puerto de la Lancha loop from Robledo de Chavela&lt;/a&gt;, might require over 7 hours to complete.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while road cycling in Robledo De Chavela?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty. You can encounter extensive oak and stone pine forests, dehesas, and riparian vegetation along the Cofio River. Notable natural landmarks include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/740607" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Seat of Philip II&lt;/a&gt;, a natural viewpoint, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2505177" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mount Abantos&lt;/a&gt;. The area is also part of the Natura 2000 network, supporting a vibrant bird population.

Q: Are there any specific viewpoints along the road cycling routes?
A: Yes, several routes offer panoramic views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6928874" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views of Hoyo de Pinares – Puerto de la Lancha loop from Robledo de Chavela&lt;/a&gt; is known for its expansive vistas. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/740607" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Seat of Philip II&lt;/a&gt; provides sweeping views of the Guadarrama Valley and Sierra de Navacerrada, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39561733" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viewpoint of the city of Madrid – Cruz Verde Pass loop from Robledo de Chavela&lt;/a&gt; offers unique perspectives.

Q: Is there parking available for cyclists in Robledo De Chavela?
A: As a hub for outdoor activities, Robledo De Chavela typically offers public parking options within the town, making it convenient to start your cycling routes. Specific parking details may vary, so it's advisable to check local signage upon arrival.

Q: How can I reach Robledo De Chavela by public transport to access the cycling routes?
A: Robledo De Chavela is located about 60 kilometers from Madrid. Public transport, such as bus services, connects the town to the capital and surrounding areas, providing an option for cyclists to reach the starting points of various routes without a car.

Q: Are there places to stop for refreshments or accommodation along the routes?
A: Many routes pass through picturesque villages and near historical sites, offering opportunities to stop for refreshments. Robledo De Chavela itself, and nearby towns like San Lorenzo de El Escorial and San Martín de Valdeiglesias, provide cafes, restaurants, and accommodation options for cyclists.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes available?
A: Yes, many of the road cycling routes in Robledo De Chavela are designed as circular loops. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e977182165/robledo-de-chavela-valdemorillo-navalagamella-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Robledo de Chavela, Valdemorillo, Navalagamella – circular route&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31521543" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cruz Verde Pass – La Escondida loop from Robledo de Chavela&lt;/a&gt;, allowing you to start and finish in the same location.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Robledo De Chavela?
A: Given its location between mountain ranges, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant conditions for road cycling, with milder temperatures and beautiful scenery. Summer can be warm, especially at lower elevations, while winter may bring colder temperatures and potential for snow in higher areas.

Q: Are the road cycling routes in Robledo De Chavela suitable for families?
A: The road cycling routes in Robledo De Chavela are generally categorized as moderate to difficult, often involving significant elevation changes and longer distances. While some sections might be manageable, families with younger children or those seeking very easy, flat rides might find these routes challenging. It's best to review individual route profiles for suitability.

Q: Can I bring my dog on road cycling routes in Robledo De Chavela?
A: Road cycling routes are typically designed for human cyclists and may not be suitable for dogs, especially given the distances, speeds, and potential traffic. While the region is environmentally protected and rich in wildlife, it's generally recommended to explore dog-friendly hiking or walking trails if you wish to bring your pet, rather than road cycling routes.

Q: How many road cycling routes are available in Robledo De Chavela?
A: There are over 20 road cycling routes available around Robledo De Chavela, offering a wide variety of distances and difficulty levels to explore the region's diverse landscapes.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy the most about road cycling in Robledo De Chavela?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 3.25 stars from over 7 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied topography, the scenic routes through oak and stone pine forests, and the challenging ascents like the Cruz Verde Pass, which offer rewarding panoramic views.
tips: Peralejo is a town of restaurants and cafeterias where you can rest for a while and recharge your batteries. It also has the Cañada Real Nature Center, where wild animals with injuries are treated. In the surrounding area there are birds of prey, day and night, eagles, vultures, wolves, etc.
Navalagamella is known for its hiking trails and natural surroundings, perfect for outdoor activities. In addition, its historical and cultural heritage offers an enriching experience for visitors.

Incredible place full of antennas and radio telescopes to listen to space. The antennas impress, for their size and for the work they do. Check their official website to see if the museum is open or not.
